raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assemblymember Al Muratsuchi called for $3 million in grants to the State Library for educational activities and materials on civil liberties and the World War II Japanese American Incarceration.",
    "The incarceration of over 120,000 Japanese Americans without any due process of law began with an Executive Order issued during World War II.",
    "President Trump is issuing executive orders targeting Muslims and refugees as national security threats, similar to how Japanese Americans were targeted during World War II.",
    "It is crucial for every American to learn the lessons of the Japanese American Incarceration to prevent future targeting based on national origin or faith.",
    "Karen Korematsu's father's case, in which he was found guilty of violating Executive Order 9066 by the U.S. Supreme Court, is being used as a warning against racist laws today.",
    "The history of Japanese Americans during World War II serves as a lesson that can educate young people and help communities understand resilience against fear and hate."
  ]
}
```
